source.source_section: Thyroid Nodules (Benign) curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate thyroid structural disease, compressive features, or thyroid inflammatory/autoimmune pattern. Goiter, tenderness, asymmetry, or a dominant nodule changes etiology and imaging decisions.
requiredExam.rationale: Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).
requiredExam.technique: Inspect and palpate the thyroid from in front or behind while the patient swallows, noting size, tenderness, asymmetry, nodules, fixation, or bruit if auscultated.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: During thyroid hormone or thyroid structural evaluation
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: thyroid structural disease, compressive features, or thyroid inflammatory/autoimmune pattern
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Goiter, tenderness, asymmetry, or a dominant nodule changes etiology and imaging decisions.
requiredExam.limitations: Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Thyroid Nodules (Benign), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.

requiredExam.rationale: Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).
requiredExam.technique: Palpate cervical and supraclavicular nodal chains systematically, noting size, tenderness, mobility, firmness, and laterality.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When thyroid nodule, thyroid cancer, goiter, neck symptoms, or autoimmune thyroid disease is relevant
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: regional nodal disease or thyroid malignancy risk

reference_values: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context.
Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay.
T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific.
FNA size thresholds are risk-system-specific; high-risk nodules are sampled at smaller sizes than low-risk nodules.
Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
decision_steps: Measure TSH first; if TSH is low, obtain radionuclide scan to identify hyperfunctioning nodules before FNA decisions.
If TSH is normal or high, perform high-quality thyroid ultrasound with risk stratification, nodule size, cervical node assessment, and compressive-symptom review.
Apply the local ATA, ACR TI-RADS, or ETA risk-system size thresholds for FNA; high-risk sonographic patterns have lower biopsy thresholds than low-risk patterns.
Use Bethesda cytology, molecular testing when locally appropriate, and ultrasound surveillance intervals to decide repeat FNA, surgery, or observation.
Confirm that Thyroid Nodules (Benign) is the selected clinical intent, then screen for red flags before routine outpatient testing.
Order the first-line tests in sequence: TSH; High-quality thyroid ultrasound with risk stratification; FNA when indicated by size/risk system; low TSH prompts radionuclide scan.

Interpret numeric results against the workup cutoffs and local assay ranges: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay.

If the initial result is equivocal, discordant with the phenotype, or affected by medication, acute illness, pregnancy, age, sex, or assay interference, repeat or confirm using the next guideline test before committing to long-term therapy.
treatment_options: Observe benign low-risk nodules with ultrasound surveillance and symptom monitoring.
Treat autonomous hyperfunctioning nodules with radioactive iodine, surgery, or ablation according to size, symptoms, pregnancy context, and local expertise.
Refer suspicious cytology, suspicious nodes, rapid growth, compressive symptoms, or high-risk history to thyroid specialist/surgery.
Consider minimally invasive ablation only for selected benign symptomatic nodules in centers with appropriate expertise.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Thyroid Nodules (Benign); draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
For confirmed Thyroid Nodules (Benign), choose therapy based on severity, comorbidities, pregnancy/fertility context, contraindications, patient preference, and local specialty pathway.
Monitor treatment response with the condition-specific clinical endpoint plus the most relevant labs or imaging listed in this workup.
Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.

source_thresholds.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: high-suspicion or intermediate-suspicion ultrasound pattern meets FNA threshold at >=1 cm
ATA thyroid nodule FNA: low-suspicion pattern >=1.5 cm; very-low-suspicion/spongiform pattern consider FNA >=2 cm or observe
ATA thyroid nodule FNA: low-suspicion pattern >=1.5 cm; very-low-suspicion/spongiform pattern consider FNA >=2 cm or observe
ATA thyroid nodule no-biopsy branch: pure cystic nodules have <1% malignancy risk and no FNA unless symptomatic/cosmetic drainage is needed

Thyroid Nodules (Benign): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup

Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay.

Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease.
Suspicious ultrasound/Bethesda cytology changes cancer pathway.
Low TSH with hot nodule shifts to autonomous-function pathway.
Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.
Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: high-suspicion or intermediate-suspicion ultrasound pattern meets FNA threshold at >=1 cm
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends diagnostic FNA for thyroid nodules >=1 cm with high-suspicion sonographic pattern and >=1 cm with intermediate-suspicion pattern.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: low-suspicion pattern >=1.5 cm; very-low-suspicion/spongiform pattern consider FNA >=2 cm or observe
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends FNA at >=1.5 cm for low-suspicion nodules and considers FNA at >=2 cm for very-low-suspicion or spongiform nodules, with observation also reasonable.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule no-biopsy branch: pure cystic nodules have <1% malignancy risk and no FNA unless symptomatic/cosmetic drainage is needed
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 Table 6 lists purely cystic nodules as benign pattern with estimated malignancy risk <1% and states no biopsy is required; aspiration may be considered for symptoms or cosmetic drainage.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: low-suspicion pattern >=1.5 cm; very-low-suspicion/spongiform pattern consider FNA >=2 cm or observe
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends FNA at >=1.5 cm for low-suspicion nodules and considers FNA at >=2 cm for very-low-suspicion or spongiform nodules, with observation also reasonable.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule no-biopsy branch: pure cystic nodules have <1% malignancy risk and no FNA unless symptomatic/cosmetic drainage is needed
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 Table 6 lists purely cystic nodules as benign pattern with estimated malignancy risk <1% and states no biopsy is required; aspiration may be considered for symptoms or cosmetic drainage.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

thyroid_nodules_review_endpoint.description: Use clinician review when Thyroid Nodules (Benign) treatment/disposition depends on pregnancy/postpartum status, pediatric/adult applicability, renal/hepatic/cardiac disease, allergy, drug interaction, procedural risk, guideline conflict, or local protocol.

thyroid_nodules_monitoring_bundle.description: Monitor Thyroid Nodules (Benign) by trending the same cited threshold/result findings that selected the branch, plus medication adverse effects and disposition constraints.

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: high-suspicion or intermediate-suspicion ultrasound pattern meets FNA threshold at >=1 cm
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends diagnostic FNA for thyroid nodules >=1 cm with high-suspicion sonographic pattern and >=1 cm with intermediate-suspicion pattern.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: low-suspicion pattern >=1.5 cm; very-low-suspicion/spongiform pattern consider FNA >=2 cm or observe
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends FNA at >=1.5 cm for low-suspicion nodules and considers FNA at >=2 cm for very-low-suspicion or spongiform nodules, with observation also reasonable.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule no-biopsy branch: pure cystic nodules have <1% malignancy risk and no FNA unless symptomatic/cosmetic drainage is needed
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 Table 6 lists purely cystic nodules as benign pattern with estimated malignancy risk <1% and states no biopsy is required; aspiration may be considered for symptoms or cosmetic drainage.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: high-suspicion or intermediate-suspicion ultrasound pattern meets FNA threshold at >=1 cm
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends diagnostic FNA for thyroid nodules >=1 cm with high-suspicion sonographic pattern and >=1 cm with intermediate-suspicion pattern.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: low-suspicion pattern >=1.5 cm; very-low-suspicion/spongiform pattern consider FNA >=2 cm or observe
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends FNA at >=1.5 cm for low-suspicion nodules and considers FNA at >=2 cm for very-low-suspicion or spongiform nodules, with observation also reasonable.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule no-biopsy branch: pure cystic nodules have <1% malignancy risk and no FNA unless symptomatic/cosmetic drainage is needed
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 Table 6 lists purely cystic nodules as benign pattern with estimated malignancy risk <1% and states no biopsy is required; aspiration may be considered for symptoms or cosmetic drainage.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

root.action: Route Thyroid Nodules (Benign) from documented symptoms, exam, vitals, results, medication/comorbidity modifiers, severity thresholds, treatment/disposition rules, reassessment, and safety-net needs.

children.label: Missing data needed: Thyroid Nodules (Benign) pathway context
children.edgeLabel: Missing exact Thyroid Nodules (Benign) pathway data: symptoms, exam, vitals, labs/results, medications, comorbidities, demographics, pregnancy/applicability, and current findings

criteria.description: Route here when the patient context needed to activate Thyroid Nodules (Benign) is absent or cannot be extracted.

children.action: Before choosing a non-emergency Thyroid Nodules (Benign) branch, document: presenting trigger, onset/trajectory, focused exam, vital signs, threshold labs/results, medication/allergy context, comorbidities, demographics, pregnancy/applicability status, and current workup findings.

children.label: Thyroid Nodules (Benign): obtain concurrent diagnostic, severity, and treatment-safety data
children.edgeLabel: Thyroid Nodules (Benign) active from documented findings: Has the thyroid nodule or neck mass grown rapidly, become painful, fixed, or associated with hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes?; Any neck swelling, rapid growth, pain, hoarseness, trouble swallowing, positional shortness of breath, radiation exposure, or family thyroid cancer/MEN2 history?; Any childhood head/neck radiation, therapeutic radiation, occupational exposure, or family thyroid cancer risk factor?; Any personal or family history of MEN, VHL, NF1, SDHx-related tumors, medullary thyroid cancer, pheochromocytoma, paraganglioma, or adrenal/pituitary tumors?; Hard fixed nodule, rapid growth, vocal cord symptoms, suspicious lymph nodes, childhood radiation, MEN2 family history.; Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.; TSH.

criteria.description: Obtain Thyroid Nodules (Benign) diagnostic/severity/treatment-safety data together rather than as a one-step chain.

children.action: Obtain together: TSH.; High-quality thyroid ultrasound with risk stratification.; FNA when indicated by size/risk system; low TSH prompts radionuclide scan.; Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions.; Structural thyroid safety workup: use TSH to route radionuclide scan versus FNA pathway, thyroid ultrasound with cervical-node mapping for suspicious nodules/cancer, calcitonin/RET context when medullary cancer or MEN2 is possible, and pregnancy testing before radioiodine decisions.; TSH with free T4; add T3 for thyrotoxicosis, TPO Ab for autoimmune hypothyroidism, TRAb/TSI for Graves, and thyroid ultrasound/FNA pathway for nodules.; Hold high-dose biotin before susceptible assays when feasible and interpret thyroid labs with pregnancy, acute illness, and central hypothyroidism context.; TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context.; Measure blood pressure; Measure heart rate; Document mental status; Measure temperature; thyroid ultrasound pattern; largest nodule dimension; extrathyroidal extension features; microcalcifications/margins/shape; lymph node findings; thyroid ultrasound pattern; largest nodule dimension; spongiform/partially cystic status.

children.label: Thyroid Nodules (Benign): emergency escalation or monitored-care disposition when danger thresholds are met
children.edgeLabel: Thyroid Nodules (Benign) danger feature or high-risk cutoff: Hard fixed nodule, rapid growth, vocal cord symptoms, suspicious lymph nodes, childhood radiation, MEN2 family history.; Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.; Thyroid storm, myxedema coma, atrial fibrillation/heart failure, vision-threatening orbitopathy, rapidly enlarging neck mass, hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes.; Measure blood pressure; >=1 cm; >=1.5 cm; >=2 cm; Suspicious ultrasound/Bethesda cytology changes cancer pathway.

criteria.description: Escalate Thyroid Nodules (Benign) when any condition-specific danger feature, high-risk cutoff, unstable physiology, or monitored-care disposition rule is present.

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: high-suspicion or intermediate-suspicion ultrasound pattern meets FNA threshold at >=1 cm
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends diagnostic FNA for thyroid nodules >=1 cm with high-suspicion sonographic pattern and >=1 cm with intermediate-suspicion pattern.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: low-suspicion pattern >=1.5 cm; very-low-suspicion/spongiform pattern consider FNA >=2 cm or observe
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends FNA at >=1.5 cm for low-suspicion nodules and considers FNA at >=2 cm for very-low-suspicion or spongiform nodules, with observation also reasonable.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule no-biopsy branch: pure cystic nodules have <1% malignancy risk and no FNA unless symptomatic/cosmetic drainage is needed
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 Table 6 lists purely cystic nodules as benign pattern with estimated malignancy risk <1% and states no biopsy is required; aspiration may be considered for symptoms or cosmetic drainage.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: high-suspicion or intermediate-suspicion ultrasound pattern meets FNA threshold at >=1 cm
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends diagnostic FNA for thyroid nodules >=1 cm with high-suspicion sonographic pattern and >=1 cm with intermediate-suspicion pattern.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: low-suspicion pattern >=1.5 cm; very-low-suspicion/spongiform pattern consider FNA >=2 cm or observe
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends FNA at >=1.5 cm for low-suspicion nodules and considers FNA at >=2 cm for very-low-suspicion or spongiform nodules, with observation also reasonable.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule no-biopsy branch: pure cystic nodules have <1% malignancy risk and no FNA unless symptomatic/cosmetic drainage is needed
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA 2015 Table 6 lists purely cystic nodules as benign pattern with estimated malignancy risk <1% and states no biopsy is required; aspiration may be considered for symptoms or cosmetic drainage.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: high-suspicion or intermediate-suspicion ultrasound pattern meets FNA threshold at >=1 cm
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends diagnostic FNA for thyroid nodules >=1 cm with high-suspicion sonographic pattern and >=1 cm with intermediate-suspicion pattern.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: low-suspicion pattern >=1.5 cm; very-low-suspicion/spongiform pattern consider FNA >=2 cm or observe
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends FNA at >=1.5 cm for low-suspicion nodules and considers FNA at >=2 cm for very-low-suspicion or spongiform nodules, with observation also reasonable.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

criteria_options.label: ATA thyroid nodule no-biopsy branch: pure cystic nodules have <1% malignancy risk and no FNA unless symptomatic/cosmetic drainage is needed
criteria_options.criteria_text: ATA 2015 Table 6 lists purely cystic nodules as benign pattern with estimated malignancy risk <1% and states no biopsy is required; aspiration may be considered for symptoms or cosmetic drainage.
criteria_options.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: high-suspicion or intermediate-suspicion ultrasound pattern meets FNA threshold at >=1 cm
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends diagnostic FNA for thyroid nodules >=1 cm with high-suspicion sonographic pattern and >=1 cm with intermediate-suspicion pattern.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: low-suspicion pattern >=1.5 cm; very-low-suspicion/spongiform pattern consider FNA >=2 cm or observe
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends FNA at >=1.5 cm for low-suspicion nodules and considers FNA at >=2 cm for very-low-suspicion or spongiform nodules, with observation also reasonable.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule no-biopsy branch: pure cystic nodules have <1% malignancy risk and no FNA unless symptomatic/cosmetic drainage is needed
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA 2015 Table 6 lists purely cystic nodules as benign pattern with estimated malignancy risk <1% and states no biopsy is required; aspiration may be considered for symptoms or cosmetic drainage.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

children.label: Thyroid Nodules (Benign): de-escalate, stop, or narrow when objective criteria normalize
children.edgeLabel: Thyroid Nodules (Benign) objective response supports narrowing, stopping, tapering, or continuing: >=1 cm; >=1.5 cm; >=2 cm; <1%; Suspicious ultrasound/Bethesda cytology changes cancer pathway.; Low TSH with hot nodule shifts to autonomous-function pathway.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Observe benign low-risk nodules with ultrasound surveillance and symptom monitoring.

criteria.description: Stop, narrow, taper, or de-escalate Thyroid Nodules (Benign) treatment only when objective response, diagnosis certainty, and cited stopping criteria support it.

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: high-suspicion or intermediate-suspicion ultrasound pattern meets FNA threshold at >=1 cm
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends diagnostic FNA for thyroid nodules >=1 cm with high-suspicion sonographic pattern and >=1 cm with intermediate-suspicion pattern.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: low-suspicion pattern >=1.5 cm; very-low-suspicion/spongiform pattern consider FNA >=2 cm or observe
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends FNA at >=1.5 cm for low-suspicion nodules and considers FNA at >=2 cm for very-low-suspicion or spongiform nodules, with observation also reasonable.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule no-biopsy branch: pure cystic nodules have <1% malignancy risk and no FNA unless symptomatic/cosmetic drainage is needed
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA 2015 Table 6 lists purely cystic nodules as benign pattern with estimated malignancy risk <1% and states no biopsy is required; aspiration may be considered for symptoms or cosmetic drainage.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

parallel_actions: Observe benign low-risk nodules with ultrasound surveillance and symptom monitoring.
Treat autonomous hyperfunctioning nodules with radioactive iodine, surgery, or ablation according to size, symptoms, pregnancy context, and local expertise.
Refer suspicious cytology, suspicious nodes, rapid growth, compressive symptoms, or high-risk history to thyroid specialist/surgery.
Consider minimally invasive ablation only for selected benign symptomatic nodules in centers with appropriate expertise.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Thyroid Nodules (Benign); draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
Suspicious ultrasound/Bethesda cytology changes cancer pathway.
Low TSH with hot nodule shifts to autonomous-function pathway.
Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: high-suspicion or intermediate-suspicion ultrasound pattern meets FNA threshold at >=1 cm
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends diagnostic FNA for thyroid nodules >=1 cm with high-suspicion sonographic pattern and >=1 cm with intermediate-suspicion pattern.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: low-suspicion pattern >=1.5 cm; very-low-suspicion/spongiform pattern consider FNA >=2 cm or observe
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends FNA at >=1.5 cm for low-suspicion nodules and considers FNA at >=2 cm for very-low-suspicion or spongiform nodules, with observation also reasonable.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule no-biopsy branch: pure cystic nodules have <1% malignancy risk and no FNA unless symptomatic/cosmetic drainage is needed
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA 2015 Table 6 lists purely cystic nodules as benign pattern with estimated malignancy risk <1% and states no biopsy is required; aspiration may be considered for symptoms or cosmetic drainage.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

children.label: Thyroid Nodules (Benign): switch to mimic/alternate pathway when threshold/result criteria do not fit
children.edgeLabel: Thyroid Nodules (Benign) mimic/exclusion better fits: Thyroid Nodules (Benign): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup; Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay.; Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease.

criteria.description: Use an alternate pathway when Thyroid Nodules (Benign) criteria are absent, a cited mimic better explains the presentation, or objective result data contradict this workup.

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: low-suspicion pattern >=1.5 cm; very-low-suspicion/spongiform pattern consider FNA >=2 cm or observe

guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule no-biopsy branch: pure cystic nodules have <1% malignancy risk and no FNA unless symptomatic/cosmetic drainage is needed

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: high-suspicion or intermediate-suspicion ultrasound pattern meets FNA threshold at >=1 cm
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends diagnostic FNA for thyroid nodules >=1 cm with high-suspicion sonographic pattern and >=1 cm with intermediate-suspicion pattern.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule FNA: low-suspicion pattern >=1.5 cm; very-low-suspicion/spongiform pattern consider FNA >=2 cm or observe
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA 2015 recommends FNA at >=1.5 cm for low-suspicion nodules and considers FNA at >=2 cm for very-low-suspicion or spongiform nodules, with observation also reasonable.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

guideline_cutoffs.label: ATA thyroid nodule no-biopsy branch: pure cystic nodules have <1% malignancy risk and no FNA unless symptomatic/cosmetic drainage is needed
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ATA 2015 Table 6 lists purely cystic nodules as benign pattern with estimated malignancy risk <1% and states no biopsy is required; aspiration may be considered for symptoms or cosmetic drainage.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Recommendation 8 and Table 6

synthetic_patient_scenarios.expected_active_branch: Missing exact Thyroid Nodules (Benign) pathway data: symptoms, exam, vitals, labs/results, medications, comorbidities, demographics, pregnancy/applicability, and current findings

Missing exact Thyroid Nodules (Benign) result(s): thyroid ultrasound pattern; largest nodule dimension; extrathyroidal extension features; microcalcifications/margins/shape; lymph node findings; spongiform/partially cystic status; patient preference/follow-up access; pure cystic status

Thyroid Nodules (Benign) danger feature or high-risk cutoff: Hard fixed nodule, rapid growth, vocal cord symptoms, suspicious lymph nodes, childhood radiation, MEN2 family history.; Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.; Thyroid storm, myxedema coma, atrial fibrillation/heart failure, vision-threatening orbitopathy, rapidly enlarging neck mass, hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes.; Measure blood pressure; >=1 cm; >=1.5 cm; >=2 cm; Suspicious ultrasound/Bethesda cytology changes cancer pathway.

Thyroid Nodules (Benign) mimic/exclusion better fits: Thyroid Nodules (Benign): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup; Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay.; Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease.

Thyroid Nodules (Benign) modifier changes treatment/disposition: Measure blood pressure; Measure heart rate; Document mental status; Measure temperature; pregnancy/postpartum/lactation status; renal/hepatic/cardiac disease; allergy or drug interaction; pediatric/adult applicability

Thyroid Nodules (Benign) reassessment worsens or contradicts expected response: >=1 cm; >=1.5 cm; >=2 cm; <1%; TSH.; High-quality thyroid ultrasound with risk stratification.; FNA when indicated by size/risk system; low TSH prompts radionuclide scan.; Measure blood pressure

Thyroid Nodules (Benign) objective response supports narrowing, stopping, tapering, or continuing: >=1 cm; >=1.5 cm; >=2 cm; <1%; Suspicious ultrasound/Bethesda cytology changes cancer pathway.; Low TSH with hot nodule shifts to autonomous-function pathway.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Observe benign low-risk nodules with ultrasound surveillance and symptom monitoring.

Thyroid Nodules (Benign) discharge/outpatient criteria satisfied: pending-result owner; follow-up interval; return precautions; Suspicious ultrasound/Bethesda cytology changes cancer pathway.; Low TSH with hot nodule shifts to autonomous-function pathway.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.
